skill.name: ddd-strategic-design
skill.description: Design DDD strategic artifacts including subdomains, bounded contexts, and ubiquitous language for complex business domains.
skill.summary: Design DDD strategic artifacts including subdomains, bounded contexts, and ubiquitous language for complex business domains.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged patterns related to backticks and YAML keys. Evaluation confirms all findings are false positives. The skill contains only markdown documentation for DDD methodology. Backticks are markdown formatting for file paths. YAML frontmatter keys like 'source:' and 'risk:' triggered cryptographic pattern detection but are metadata fields. No executable code, network requests, or security concerns detected.

content.user_title: Map Domain Boundaries with DDD Strategic Design
content.value_statement: This skill helps you design Domain-Driven Design strategic artifacts like subdomains, bounded contexts, and ubiquitous language to create clear domain boundaries and shared understanding with domain experts.
actual_capabilities: Classify subdomains as core, supporting, or generic based on business value
Define bounded contexts around consistency boundaries and team ownership
Create ubiquitous language glossaries with canonical terms and anti-terms
Map context boundaries with upstream and downstream dependencies
Generate subdomain classification tables with owner teams and rationale
Document bounded context catalogs with responsibilities and integrations
limitations: Cannot infer business truth without stakeholder input
Does not provide tactical DDD patterns (entities, aggregates, value objects)
Requires domain expert collaboration for accurate results

use_cases.title: Architectural Design Sessions
use_cases.description: Use during architecture workshops to map domain boundaries and define bounded contexts for a new microservices initiative.

use_cases.title: Monolith Decomposition
use_cases.description: Apply when splitting a monolith to identify subdomain boundaries and determine which components belong in each bounded context.

use_cases.title: Team Ownership Mapping
use_cases.description: Establish clear team ownership and responsibility boundaries using bounded context cataloging.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Subdomain Mapping
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @ddd-strategic-design to help me identify subdomains in our [domain name] domain. We have capabilities for [list key capabilities]. Please classify each as core, supporting, or generic.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner users who need to identify and classify subdomains

prompt_templates.title: Bounded Context Definition
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @ddd-strategic-design to define bounded contexts for our [domain]. We have identified these subdomains: [list subdomains]. Please help create bounded contexts with clear ownership boundaries.
prompt_templates.scenario: Users who have identified subdomains and need to define contexts

prompt_templates.title: Ubiquitous Language Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @ddd-strategic-design to build a ubiquitous language glossary. For our [bounded context], we need to define these terms: [list terms]. Include definitions and identify any conflicting meanings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Teams establishing shared vocabulary with domain experts

prompt_templates.title: Complete Strategic Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @ddd-strategic-design to perform a complete strategic design for our [domain name] domain. Include subdomain classification, bounded context catalog with dependencies, and ubiquitous language glossary. Our key capabilities are: [list capabilities].
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive DDD strategic design from scratch

output_examples.input: Use @ddd-strategic-design to map our e-commerce domain into bounded contexts, classify subdomains, and propose team ownership.
output_examples.output:
## Subdomain Classification

| Capability | Type | Rationale | Owner |
|------------|------|------------|-------|
| Product Catalog | Core | Differentiates business | Commerce Team |
| User Authentication | Supporting | Required but not unique | Platform Team |
| Email Delivery | Generic | Utility function | Platform Team |

## Bounded Contexts

| Context | Responsibility | Upstream | Downstream |
|---------|----------------|----------|------------|
| Catalog | Product data | Suppliers | Checkout, Search |
| Checkout | Orders | Catalog | Fulfillment |

## Ubiquitous Language

| Term | Definition | Context |
|------|-------------|---------|
| Order | Confirmed purchase | Checkout |
| SKU | Stock keeping unit | Catalog |

output_examples.input: Use @ddd-strategic-design to help us identify subdomains in our healthcare domain with patient records, appointments, billing, and insurance verification.
output_examples.output:
## Subdomain Classification

| Capability | Type | Rationale | Owner |
|------------|------|------------|-------|
| Patient Records | Core | Clinical differentiation | Clinical Team |
| Appointments | Supporting | Required operations | Operations Team |
| Billing | Core | Revenue differentiation | Finance Team |
| Insurance Verification | Supporting | Enablement | Revenue Team |

best_practices: Involve domain experts in subdomain classification to ensure accurate business value assessment
Start with capability mapping before diving into bounded contexts for clearer boundaries
Document boundary decisions with explicit rationale in ADRs before implementation
anti_patterns: Creating too many bounded contexts which introduces unnecessary complexity and integration overhead
Ignoring upstream and downstream dependencies when defining context boundaries
Skipping ubiquitous language creation and assuming everyone shares the same vocabulary

faq.question: What is the difference between subdomains and bounded contexts?
faq.answer: Subdomains are problem space concepts representing business capabilities. Bounded contexts are solution space implementations that encapsulate a domain model. Multiple subdomains may map to one bounded context, or one subdomain may span multiple contexts.

faq.question: When should I use this skill versus DDD tactical patterns?
faq.answer: Use this skill for strategic design work: defining boundaries, ownership, and language. Use tactical patterns (entities, aggregates, value objects) after strategic design is complete to implement the inside of each bounded context.

faq.question: How many bounded contexts should a system have?
faq.answer: There is no fixed number. Start with fewer contexts and split when you find strong teams needing independent models or different release cadences. Too many contexts create integration overhead; too few cause unnecessary coupling.

faq.question: Can this skill help with microservices architecture?
faq.answer: Yes. Strategic design is the first step in microservices decomposition. This skill helps identify natural service boundaries based on domain boundaries rather than technical layering.

faq.question: What if domain experts disagree on terminology?
faq.answer: Document conflicting definitions in your ubiquitous language as anti-terms. Use bounded contexts to isolate contexts with conflicting definitions. This is a common DDD pattern called context mapping.

faq.question: Does this skill work for small projects?
faq.answer: For small projects with stable domains and single teams, strategic design may be overkill. The skill is most valuable when complexity, multiple teams, or domain complexity justifies the investment.
